text: Work on the lexintel pipeline plugin centered on formalizing run state and the case/hypothesis apparatus: defining pipeline- and step-level run states (not-ready, ready, running, complete, error, stale), aligning run folder structure and the case registry format, and a workspace-clear command that resets all steps to not-run. Substantial dashboard work on pipeline.html â€” fixed a 404 in the _status polling endpoint, addressed severe UI lag in the event/polling loop, added a role-level shading scheme with a legend for staleness, a collapsible per-contract dependency graph (collapsed by default, folded into an expandable description), and a Zoomable full-graph view with a collapsible right panel. Introduced proper typing in the engine so step-returning methods yield real Step runtime objects, and restructured the Step data type into explicit front-matter vs. sections (description, input, tools, outputs, instructions) after the user pushed back hard on ad-hoc variable-bag validation. Ran pipeline steps A1-A5 on the HOTEL case and regenerated the memo, and prepped onboarding for a collaborator (Michael) including the git pull-request flow. Ended mid-diagnosis of a stale-data bug: the dashboard reads a step's output sidecar JSON that belongs to a prior run because a step was re-run before the old output was deleted and the deletion never happened â€” the user was calling out this ordering/cleanup rookie error when the session cut off.
